title: Fix no-op assertion in watch test
description:
The watch test has a no-op assertion:

  assert!(!events.is_empty() || true); // Platform-dependent

This always passes. Either make the test conditional on platform, remove it, or implement proper platform-specific expectations.

Location: src/storage/watch.rs:114-116
close_reason: Implemented

title: Add message read tracking with --after-offset and mark-read command
description:
Track which messages agents have seen to avoid re-reading.

Design (stateless + persistent fallback):
1. history --after-offset=<bytes> - agent passes offset from context
2. history --after-id=<ulid> - robust alternative using message ID
3. mark-read <channel> --offset=<bytes> - persist read cursor
4. inbox <channel> - get only unread messages (uses stored offset)

Per-agent state stored in .botbus/agents/<AgentName>/state.json to avoid conflicts when multiple agents share a project.

This helps agents avoid re-processing messages after context compaction.
close_reason: Implemented message read tracking with inbox and mark-read commands

title: TUI: Fix message scrolling - viewport shrinks instead of scrolling
description: When pressing k/j to scroll, messages disappear from bottom/top but the viewport shrinks instead of maintaining size and shifting all messages. The window should stay the same height and all visible messages should move together.
close_reason: Fixed: clamped scroll offset to max_scroll (messages.len() - inner_height) so viewport maintains constant height when scrolled to oldest messages

title: Use BOTBUS_AGENT env var for identity instead of state file
description:
Current design stores 'current_agent' in .botbus/state.json, which breaks when multiple agents work from the same directory. 

Solution:
1. register command outputs: export BOTBUS_AGENT=AgentName
2. All commands read identity from BOTBUS_AGENT env var (with --agent flag override)
3. whoami reads from env var, not state file
4. Remove current_agent from state.json

This matches how mcp_agent_mail works - identity flows through the session/process, not shared state.
close_reason: Implemented BOTBUS_AGENT env var and --agent flag for identity. All commands now use resolve_agent() which checks explicit flag, env var, then legacy state file.

title: Add README.md
close_reason: Added

title: Fix state file update race condition
description:
ProjectState::update() and AgentStateManager::update() have a race condition. The lock is released between load() and save(), allowing concurrent updates to overwrite each other.

Location: src/storage/state.rs:128-136, src/storage/agent_state.rs:132-139

Fix: Hold exclusive lock across the entire read-modify-write operation.

Severity: Medium - parallel agent operations can lose state updates.
close_reason: Fixed by holding exclusive lock across entire read-modify-write operation in update() methods

title: Implement storage layer (JSONL read/write with locking)
close_reason: Storage layer complete: JSONL read/write with locking, state management, file watching

title: Add claim TTL extension
description:
If an agent's claim is about to expire but work isn't done, there's no way to extend it without releasing and re-claiming. This creates a window where another agent could claim the same files.

Suggestion: Add 'botbus claim --extend <pattern>' to bump TTL on existing claims owned by the current agent.
close_reason: Implemented

title: check-claim should exit non-zero on conflict
description:
check_claim() returns Result<bool> but the CLI doesn't propagate the boolean to exit code. For shell scripting (e.g., 'botbus check-claim src/foo.rs && edit src/foo.rs'), exit code should be non-zero on conflict.

Location: src/cli/claim.rs:292-361

Fix: In main.rs command dispatch, check the bool return and exit with code 1 if false.

title: TUI: Fix scroll buffering bug - k presses accumulate
description:
When pressing k multiple times to scroll up in messages, the scroll offset keeps incrementing even when already at the top. This means you have to press j the same number of times to scroll back down, even though there's no more content to show.

The scroll offset should be clamped to the actual content bounds so pressing k at the top is a no-op.
close_reason: Fixed by clamping scroll in UI after viewport height is known

title: Add 'botbus agentsmd' subcommand for AGENTS.md management
description:
Add a 'botbus agentsmd' subcommand (like br/maw have) that can:
- Generate a BotBus section with usage instructions
- Add the section to an existing agents file
- Update an existing BotBus section to the latest version
- Remove the BotBus section
- Check whether a section exists and its status

**File detection**: Auto-detect the project's agent instructions file by looking for (in order):
- AGENTS.md
- CLAUDE.md
- .claude/settings.md
- Other common variations (agents.md, claude.md, etc.)

Use marker comments (e.g. <!-- botbus-agent-instructions-v1 --> ... <!-- end-botbus-agent-instructions -->) to delimit the section for reliable updates.

Should support:
- --add: Add instructions to the detected file
- --remove: Remove instructions
- --update: Update instructions to latest version
- --check: Check status only (default)
- --dry-run: Preview changes without modifying
- --force/-f: Skip confirmation prompts
- --file <path>: Explicitly specify the file instead of auto-detecting
- show subcommand or --show flag to print the content that would be added
close_reason: Implemented botbus agentsmd subcommand with --add, --remove, --update, --check, --show, --dry-run, --force, and --file options. Auto-detects AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, etc.
